PyTorch Training Loop: A standard PyTorch training loop iterates over minibatches, performs a forward pass to compute predictions and loss, calls loss.backward() to compute gradients, and then calls optimizer.step() to update model weights. The optimizer.zero_grad() call before each forward pass is critical to prevent gradient accumulation across batches. Validation should be performed with torch.no_grad() to disable autograd and reduce memory usage. Checkpointing model state with torch.save at the end of each epoch allows training to resume after interruption.
